title: urvillea cuchujaquensis
intro: Urvillea cuchujaquensis, also known as the Queñua del Chaco, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in the Chaco region of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Urvillea cuchujaquensis include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Urvillea cuchujaquensis is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
